required_edits: Restore or explicitly justify the AM-41(c) resolver fixture semantics: the frozen text requires a candidate with a symlink component FOLLOWED BY A LATER `..` that would normalize it away to be REJECTED because the symlink is lstat-checked in original component order. In util.mjs `originalChainSymlinkFree`, a `..` segment is processed as `cur = path.dirname(cur)` BEFORE the symlink is entered when the specifier ends with the collapse (e.g. `./link/../x.mjs` DOES check `link` — good), but the test in test-util.mjs section 16 accepts EITHER `symlink` OR `escape` as the rejection kind (`r.kind === "symlink" || r.kind === "escape"`). The frozen requirement is rejection BECAUSE of the symlink component; accepting `escape` would let an implementation that never lstats the original chain pass by coincidental containment failure. Tighten the assertion to require kind === "symlink" (or prove escape cannot occur for this fixture and document why).

Align the git wrapper allowlist with the frozen shape list or justify the widening: gitSpawnOptions spreads caller `options` FIRST and then overrides cwd/shell/encoding/stdio/maxBuffer — this is correct for the security-relevant keys, but it still forwards arbitrary other caller-supplied spawn options (env, timeout, uid, windowsHide, etc.) to execFileSync. The task text says the wrapper 'permits only the exact subcommands and argument shapes' and uses `execFileSync('git', args, {cwd: repoRoot, ...})`; forwarding an uncontrolled options bag (e.g. a caller-supplied `env` with GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE, or `argv0`) can redirect which repository is inspected without violating the arg-shape allowlist. Either freeze the accepted options to a closed set (drop unknown keys) or add a unit proving repository-redirecting env vars cannot pass through, and record the decision.

PACKAGE_ROOTS completeness unit (test-inventory section 7) invokes `execFileSync("git", ["ls-files", ...])` directly — a raw git call outside any allowlist, in a test that authoritatively proves an inventory invariant. The Task 2 fixture builder is granted 'its own separate test-only allowlist'; this test has none. Add the test-only allowlist (or route through a validated test-git helper) so the mechanically-enforcing unit itself follows the no-shell/closed-shape discipline, and pin the exact ls-files invocation shape.

The scanExports Phase-1 grammar accepts `export const NAME` with NO initializer follow-through check beyond `=?` in the `phase1` regex — `export const x;` (illegal JS but lexically matchable) and, more importantly, `export const x = 1, y = 2;` seeds ONLY `x` and silently drops `y` with no warning. The frozen grammar is 'export const followed by an identifier', so seeding x is defensible, but the silent drop of the second declarator is neither a seeded symbol nor a warned unsupported form — it is unclassified symbol flow. Add a warning (or a fatal) for multi-declarator export const, plus a unit pinning the chosen behavior.
hard_stops: Committed-closure equality is asserted through a boolean harness that swallows ALL derivation errors: in test-closure.mjs, `inventoryMatches` wraps `deriveAcceptedClosure` in `try/catch { return false }`, and section 1 asserts `inventoryMatches(...) === true`. This is fine for the positive direction, BUT the normative committed-inventory check (section 1) and the direct equality check (section 12) both derive the closure of git.mjs/code.mjs against the REAL repo — while scanExports/scanImports/classifyModuleLoads THROW ProfileError on any out-of-profile byte. If a future edit makes a weaver source out-of-profile, section 12's assert.deepEqual would fail loudly (good), so this is survivable — however the WEAVER_IMPL_FILES table itself omits `clotho/inventory.mjs` from BOTH weaver closures while the frozen Task 4a text names `clotho/inventory.mjs` as exemplary shared substrate 'such as clotho/weavers/util.mjs, clotho/registry.mjs, and clotho/inventory.mjs' participating in closures. Neither git.mjs nor code.mjs imports inventory.mjs, so the DERIVED closures legitimately exclude it and equality holds — but then NO committed Task-4a artifact proves inventory.mjs itself is within the enforced source profile or closure-clean, and the driver that WILL import it lands only in Task 5. Commit an explicit unit (e.g. deriveAcceptedClosure over inventory.mjs proving it is a profile-clean closure leaf, as done for registry.mjs in section 12) so the inventory module the whole task exists to establish is itself provably inside the discipline it freezes.

The git weaver's -S argument construction (`-S${symbol}`) is attacker-influenceable only through seeded symbols, and seeded symbols are constrained to identifier tokens by scanExports + registry's IDENTIFIER check — but git.mjs performs NO local validation that `symbol` is identifier-shaped before splicing it into the argv token, and weave() accepts ctx from the (future Task 5) driver as plain data. A ctx with symbol='x --all' would produce argv token '-Sx --all' (still one token, safe) but symbol strings containing NUL or beginning with '-' interplay with validateGitArgs only IF the runner is the wrapped one; test contexts inject raw mocks. Since the frozen text requires 'Identifier matching uses tokens or escaped guards', add a fail-closed identifier-shape check on symbol (and canonical-path check on path) INSIDE git.mjs weave() so the weaver's own contract does not depend on which runner the driver injects.
rationale: The implementation is strong and unusually disciplined: the frozen REQUIRED_INVENTORY_IDS table and LOADER_CAPABLE_BUILTIN_SAFE_EXPORTS mapping are committed exactly with deep-freeze + mutation units; AM-40 PACKAGE_ROOTS/EXCLUDE with a mechanical tracked-package.json completeness proof; one shared D33 classifier/resolver in util.mjs with AM-34 test-19 single-definition enforcement; the AM-41 profile with all six fail-closed classes (b1–b6) individually exercised; per-form closure omission fixtures covering every accepted form including require-style and .cjs traversal; git log shapes byte-exact with earliest-selection, CRLF-vs-bare-CR, and malformed-output units; code weaver covering all four depends-on endpoint shapes, no-export preservation, dedup, unused-import, comment/string-safety, and unrepresentable-consumer narrowed to genuinely unresolvable specifiers; counted-iterator semantics (partial-not-counted, exhaustion-only-on-completion, driver-held accessor) proven; D21 containment with junction-based symlink fixtures that must run. Determinism is proven byte-equal over two runs for both weavers. The deterministic gate passed. However I cannot sign approve as-is. Two items rise to hard stops: (1) inventory.mjs — the very artifact this task exists to commit — is proven neither profile-clean nor closure-analyzed by any committed unit (it is legitimately absent from both weaver closures since neither imports it, and no analogue of the section-12 registry-leaf assertion covers it), leaving the central committed inventory outside the discipline the PR freezes until Task 5; (2) git.mjs splices ctx-supplied symbol/path values into git argv without any local identifier/path-shape validation, relying entirely on the injected runner's allowlist — which the tests themselves bypass with raw mocks — contrary to the frozen 'tokens or escaped guards' matching discipline. The required edits are genuine faithfulness gaps of smaller radius: the AM-41(c) fixture accepts an alternative rejection kind the frozen text does not permit; the git wrapper forwards uncontrolled spawn options (env can redirect repository identity) under a task line that freezes the option surface; the completeness unit itself shells raw git outside any allowlist; and multi-declarator export const silently drops trailing declarators without warning, violating the warn-and-emit-nothing rule for unsupported symbol flow. None of these require reinterpretation of scope — each is fixable strictly within Task 4a's frozen text.
